title: Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean

seo.imageAlt: A freshly baked loaf of Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean with a golden crust and creamy filling.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the irresistible sweet and savory flavors of homemade Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean.

description: Indulge in the viral sensation of Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread, a delightful fusion of sweet cream cheese filling and a rich garlic butter glaze. This street food favorite features a crispy exterior and a soft, flavorful interior, making it an irresistible treat for any time of day. Perfect for sharing or enjoying as a decadent snack, this recipe brings the authentic taste of Korea right to your kitchen.

ingredients.item: Round loaf of bread or individual buns

instructions.title: Prepare the Bread
instructions.text: If using a round loaf, slice it into 6-8 wedges, making sure not to cut all the way through the bottom so it remains connected. If using individual buns, make deep cross cuts on top.

instructions.title: Make the Cream Cheese Filling
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the softened cream cheese, granulated sugar, and 30ml of heavy cream. Mix thoroughly with a spatula or whisk until the mixture is completely smooth and creamy. Transfer this filling into a piping bag.

instructions.title: Fill the Bread
instructions.text: Carefully pipe the cream cheese filling into each of the cuts you made in the bread. Ensure each wedge or section is generously filled with the creamy mixture, pushing it deep into the crevices.

instructions.title: Prepare the Garlic Butter Glaze
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, whisk together the melted unsalted butter, minced garlic, honey, 30ml of heavy cream, and the egg yolk until well combined. Stir in 1 teaspoon of dried parsley.

instructions.title: Glaze the Bread
instructions.text: Dip each cream cheese-filled bread wedge or bun into the garlic butter glaze, making sure all cut surfaces are thoroughly coated. Alternatively, you can use a brush to generously coat the entire surface of the bread with the glaze.

instructions.title: Bake the Bread
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 160C (325F). Place the glazed bread on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the bread is golden brown and emits a fragrant aroma.

instructions.title: Serve Warm
instructions.text: Once baked, carefully remove the bread from the oven. Garnish with an extra sprinkle of fresh or dried parsley before serving. Enjoy this sweet and savory delight while it's still warm.

tips: For the best texture, ensure your cream cheese is at room temperature before mixing to achieve a smooth, lump-free filling that pipes easily.
If you do not have a piping bag, you can use a resealable plastic bag with one corner snipped off to pipe the cream cheese filling into the bread cuts.
Serve the bread immediately after baking for the ultimate warm, gooey cream cheese and crispy, fragrant garlic butter experience.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the vibrant streets and bustling bakeries of Korea right from your kitchen with one of the most irresistible treats to emerge from Korean baking: Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean. This isn't just any garlic bread; it's a sensational fusion of sweet, savory, creamy, and garlicky flavors, all packed into a soft, fluffy bun. A true viral sensation, this bread has captured hearts worldwide, and for good reason. It’s a delightful snack, a perfect breakfast item, or a decadent side dish that promises an explosion of taste with every bite. If you've been dreaming of recreating this iconic treat, you've come to the right place. Get ready to master the art of making authentic Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean.

## What is Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean?
At its core, Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean is a sweet and savory bread that originated in South Korea and quickly became a street food and bakery staple. Unlike traditional Western garlic bread, which is often crusty and solely savory, the Korean version offers a harmonious blend of textures and tastes. It typically features a soft, slightly sweet bun (often brioche-like) that is deeply cut into six or eight wedges, but not all the way through. These cuts are then generously filled with a sweetened cream cheese mixture. The entire bun is then dipped or brushed lavishly with a rich, aromatic garlic butter glaze, which often includes a hint of sweetness from honey or sugar, and a creamy element from milk or egg. The result is an incredibly moist, flavorful bread that is crispy on the outside from baking, and wonderfully soft and creamy on the inside. It’s the perfect balance of garlic’s pungency, cream cheese’s tang, and a subtle sweetness that makes it utterly addictive.

## Ingredients You'll Need
Creating this masterpiece is simpler than you might think, especially if you opt for store-bought buns. Here’s what you’ll need:

For the Bread:
*   **6 round buns or dinner rolls:** Brioche buns are ideal for their softness and slight sweetness, but any soft, fluffy white bread rolls will work beautifully.

For the Cream Cheese Filling:
*   **8 ounces (226g) cream cheese:** Full-fat, softened at room temperature for easy mixing.
*   **2-3 tablespoons granulated sugar:** Adjust to your sweetness preference.
*   **1-2 tablespoons milk or heavy cream:** To achieve a smooth, pipeable consistency.
*   **1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract (optional):** For an added layer of flavor.

For the Garlic Butter Glaze:
*   **1/2 cup (113g) unsalted butter:** Melted.
*   **6-8 cloves garlic:** Freshly minced for the best flavor. Don't skimp on the garlic!
*   **2 tablespoons granulated sugar or honey:** Balances the savory notes.
*   **2 tablespoons milk or heavy cream:** Adds richness and helps emulsify the sauce.
*   **1 large egg:** Lightly beaten, helps create a beautiful golden crust.
*   **2 tablespoons dried parsley flakes:** For color and a fresh herb aroma.
*   **Pinch of salt:** To enhance flavors.

Equipment:
*   Sharp knife
*   Pastry brush
*   Baking sheet lined with parchment paper
*   Piping bag (optional, but recommended for the cream cheese filling)

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean
Follow these steps to bring your Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean dreams to life:

1.  **Prepare the Buns:** Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Place your buns on a parchment-lined baking sheet. Using a sharp knife, make six or eight deep cuts into each bun, radiating from the center outwards, like spokes on a wheel. Be careful not to cut all the way through the bottom; you want the bun to stay intact.
2.  **Make the Cream Cheese Filling:** In a medium bowl, combine the softened cream cheese, sugar, milk (or cream), and vanilla extract (if using). Mix with a spatula or electric mixer until completely smooth and creamy. Transfer the filling to a piping bag (or a Ziploc bag with a corner snipped off).
3.  **Fill the Buns:** Carefully open up the cuts in each bun and generously pipe the cream cheese filling into each crevice. You want each section to be well-filled but avoid overfilling, as it can ooze out during baking.
4.  **Prepare the Garlic Butter Glaze:** In another bowl, whisk together the melted butter, minced garlic, sugar (or honey), milk (or cream), lightly beaten egg, dried parsley, and a pinch of salt. Whisk vigorously until all ingredients are well combined and the mixture is emulsified.
5.  **Glaze the Buns:** This is a crucial step for authentic Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean. You can either dip each entire bun into the garlic butter glaze, ensuring all sides and cuts are thoroughly coated, or use a pastry brush to liberally brush the glaze over and into every part of the bread. Don't be shy – the more glaze, the more flavorful and moist your bread will be!
6.  **Bake:** Place the glazed buns back on the baking sheet. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the bread is golden brown and the garlic butter is bubbling. The cream cheese filling should be warm and slightly melted.
7.  **Serve:** Remove from the oven and let cool slightly before serving. Garnish with a sprinkle of fresh parsley if desired.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results
*   **Quality Bread Matters:** While any soft bun works, a good quality brioche bun will elevate your Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean to another level. Its inherent sweetness and richness perfectly complement the other flavors.
*   **Room Temperature Cream Cheese:** Ensure your cream cheese is completely softened. This prevents lumps and makes for a smooth, easy-to-pipe filling.
*   **Fresh Garlic is Key:** Skip the jarred minced garlic. Freshly minced garlic provides a much more potent and aromatic flavor that is essential for this recipe.
*   **Don't Skimp on the Glaze:** The garlic butter glaze is where a lot of the magic happens. Make sure the buns are thoroughly coated, allowing the glaze to seep into all the cuts.
*   **Watch Your Oven:** Ovens can vary. Keep an eye on your bread during the last few minutes of baking to prevent burning, especially the garlic. The goal is a golden-brown exterior and a soft, warm interior.
*   **Serve Warm:** Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean is best enjoyed warm, straight from the oven, when the cream cheese is gooey and the garlic butter is fragrant.

## Serving Suggestions
This versatile bread can be enjoyed in numerous ways. It’s absolutely fantastic on its own as a hearty snack or a unique breakfast item alongside a cup of coffee or tea. Many enjoy it as part of a brunch spread, offering a delightful sweet and savory option. While rich enough to be a meal by itself, it also pairs surprisingly well with lighter dishes, such as a fresh green salad, or as a decadent side to a simple soup. No matter how you serve it, its irresistible aroma will draw everyone to the table.

## Variations and Substitutions
Once you've mastered the classic Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean, feel free to experiment with these variations:

*   **Add a Spicy Kick:** For those who love a bit of heat, sprinkle some red pepper flakes into your garlic butter glaze.
*   **Herbaceous Twists:** Beyond parsley, consider adding finely chopped fresh chives or a hint of dried oregano to the glaze for different aromatic profiles.
*   **Cheesy Topping:** Before baking, sprinkle a little mozzarella or Parmesan cheese over the top for an extra layer of cheesy goodness and a crispy crust.
*   **Sweetness Adjustment:** Feel free to adjust the amount of sugar or honey in both the cream cheese filling and the garlic butter glaze to suit your palate.
*   **Different Breads:** While round buns are traditional, you can try this recipe with sliced baguettes (cut thick), or even larger dinner rolls. The key is a soft, absorbent bread.
*   **Dairy-Free Version:** For dietary restrictions, substitute dairy cream cheese with a vegan cream cheese alternative, and use plant-based milk and butter.

Making Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean at home is a rewarding experience that brings a taste of authentic Korean baking to your kitchen. With its perfect balance of sweet, savory, and garlicky goodness, this viral sensation is sure to become a beloved treat in your home. So gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to indulge in this incredibly delicious and satisfying bread!
